name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
def: A tree keyed by sequence positions, where the path from the root spells a key prefix.
problem: Fast prefix queries, autocomplete and ordered string sets without rehashing whole keys.
mechanism: Each node has one child per possible symbol; descending consumes one symbol of the key. Compressed (radix/PATRICIA) tries merge single-child chains.
benefits: O(k) lookup in key length, independent of n
failures: Memory blow-up with sparse wide-alphabet tries
Slower than a hash map for plain exact lookup
whenNot: Only exact-match lookups needed (hash table wins)
myths: "Tries are always memory-efficient" — naive tries can use far more memory than hash sets
